transcript: Who's ready for some alpha? We're gonna be talking about payments on Solana. Um, cool. Yeah. Uh, my name's Jon, Co-Founder and CEO of Artemis. Uh, we... we are a very much fintech stablecoin nerd data team. Uh, we serve Tether, Circle, Visa, PayPal. And I'm about to unveil some really cool alpha for this room that I think you guys will appreciate. So, um, question. How do we actually know that Solana is winning in payments? We see that Solana is partnering up with Cash App, which does 280 billion dollars of volume every single year, and Western Union that does 150 billion, but like, how do we track that payments are actually moving on-chain? So in the fintech world, we have this metric called Total Payment Volume. And Stripe reports every year that they do, you know, more recently 1.4 trillion dollars a year in Total Payment Volume. So how do we actually get that in crypto? And the secret is you cannot get it on-chain. So Artemis, we're the only data provider in the whole world where we work with payment service providers, exchanges, on-the-ground stablecoin players to calculate how much of volume on-chain is actually real payments. And this is not new alpha, but um, we released earlier this fall that there's about 120 billion of payment volume that's happening on-chain. And B2B payments is growing the fastest, nearly 4x year-on-year. And this is a chart that you can find on stablecoin.fyi. So this is a report we did earlier this fall. So the question is like, how do we calculate Total Payment Volume on-chain? Like, how do we take what Stripe is doing to on-chain and calculate that? So basically the TLDR of our methodology for Total Payment Volume for blockchains like Solana, we look at payroll disbursements, so like payroll-like types of activity, cross-border payments, which is a big part of uh stablecoin activity, domestic transfers, card processor volumes, peer-to-peer activity. And what we remove are things like DeFi, trading, airdrops, uh MEV, that type of stuff. So that way we like actually get real payment volume on-chain. And so, so here's the alpha. So, first unveiled here at Breakpoint. Uh, if you look at Solana today, this fall, uh Solana is approximately 5 to 6 percent of all B2B payments that are happening on-chain. Um, and stay tuned for some more. We'll be launching an official dashboard out in Q1 so you can track real payment volume across all the different chains. And so, the so what? Why are we here? Why do we care about what we're doing? Um, I think this is a pretty important slide. The Western Union CEO was saying, 'Hey look, we ended up picking Solana because they were just the right choice.' And I think we similarly view that uh in order to see that Solana is actually winning this war and winning payments and a lot of real activity is moving on-chain, the only way to do that is with TPV. Um, if this interests you, shoot me an email. We're gonna be launching in Q1. Thanks so much. Appreciate it.

sources.title: Metaplex
sources.snippet:
[Back to Blog](https://metaplex.com/blog)

## October Highlights

- **Genesis**\- Genesis powered two presales in October, accounting for 18% of the total monthly revenue, the highest share since inception.

- **Protocol Revenue** \- Protocol revenue reached $2.0M in October.

- **$MPLX Buyback**\- $1.1M of Metaplex protocol fees were used to buy back 5.3M $MPLX (0.5% of total supply / 0.9% of outstanding supply) for the Metaplex DAO.


## Pump.Fun Update (November)

On November 7th, Pump.Fun [disclosed in its developer documentation](https://github.com/pump-fun/pump-public-docs/blob/main/README.md) that it would begin minting tokens using its new Mayhem Mode through SPL Token-22 rather than Metaplex’s Token Metadata. This change went into effect on November 12th and since then the percentage of total tokens launched daily by Pump.Fun that use Metaplex’s Token Metadata has declined to less than 15%.

Metaplex Foundation expects this to result in a decline in Metaplex protocol revenue near term with Pump.Fun contributing 44%, 60%, and 45% of total revenue during August, September, and October, respectively, after peaking at 86% in January 2025.

In light of the slowdown in memecoin activity, over the last few months the Metaplex Foundation has intentionally supported the expansion of the protocol from being purely an asset standard to a broader platform for Internet Capital Markets on Solana. Genesis, Metaplex’s protocol for fair onchain token launches, has found product market fit with over $1.1M in revenue generated since its launch in July 2025 and is now scaling. Metaplex is well positioned to become the canonical end-to-end platform for launching, discovering, and trading new assets on Solana.

The Metaplex Foundation remains well funded with more than three years of operating runway to support the Metaplex ecosystem even accounting for the loss of Pump.Fun associated revenue. This excludes the value of Foundation's and the Metaplex DAO's MPLX positions, which collectively account for more than 40% of the total supply. While this change will have a significant impact on revenue, the concentration risk was well understood, requiring no significant changes in the Metaplex Foundation’s operating plans or capital allocation.

Please note that the October revenue figures that follow in the rest of the newsletter below include revenue from Pump.Fun given the change occurred in November.

## October Protocol Activity

In October, Metaplex generated $2.0M in protocol revenue including $1.6M from Token Metadata (78%), $370K from Genesis (18%), and $72K from Core (4%). Genesis accounted for 18% of total revenue, its highest share since inception.

Combined, token creation (TM tokens) and token launches (Genesis) accounted for 91% of revenue while 9% was driven by the creation of NFTs (TM NFTs, Core, BG v2).

In October, 766K Token Metadata assets were created, including 720K fungible tokens (94%).

Core adoption increased in October, with 238K mints driving the cumulative mint total over 4M.

In October, creators and developers minted over 4M digital assets through Metaplex, including 3.0M compressed NFTs. This activity raised the protocol’s cumulative total to over 960M assets minted to date.

Unique user activity continued in October, with approximately 420K unique wallets authorizing transactions powered by Metaplex.Approximately 290K new wallets collected a Token Metadata asset and over 570K new wallets collected a Bubblegum NFT in the month of October.

## Developer Platform

**Genesis**

October marked another strong month for Genesis, powering multiple token launches and expanding its role across gaming, DeFi, and consumer apps.

[The month began with PlaySolana ($PLAY)](https://x.com/metaplex/status/1977434437748150707), a consumer gaming company building portable hardware devices and an onchain gaming ecosystem. [Their fixed-price presale sold out within minutes, selling over $2.6M (13.2K SOL) to more than 1,600 participant

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

sources.title: Introducing Hermes 4.3: Local Intelligence Globally Trained
sources.snippet:
Introducing Hermes 4.3: Local Intelligence Globally Trained - NOUS RESEARCH[Skip to content](#content)
[](#elementor-action:action=popup:open&settings=eyJpZCI6IjE3NjEiLCJ0b2dnbGUiOmZhbHNlfQ==)
Menu
# Introducing Hermes 4.3: Local Intelligence Globally Trained
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/435_b_s_2-scaled.jpg)
Today we’re releasing Hermes 4.3 ([🤗 Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-4.3-36B)), an update to our flagship Hermes series of models. Hermes 4.3 was trained with an extended context length (up to 512K) and nearly matches (and in some cases exceeds) the performance of Hermes 4 70B at half the parameter cost. Based on[Seed-OSS-36B-Base](https://huggingface.co/ByteDance-Seed/Seed-OSS-36B-Base), Hermes 4.3 is an excellent shape for consumer local inference or enterprise self-deployment. The[GGUFs](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-4.3-36B-GGUF)comfortably sit in the VRAM of off-the-shelf GPUs, offering a private, powerful, neutrally-aligned model to everyone.
Hermes 4.3 is our first production model post-trained entirely on the[Psyche](https://psyche.network/)network, our distributed training network that uses the DisTrO optimizer to efficiently communicate between training nodes spread out through data centers over the open internet and secured by the consensus of the Solana blockchain. By enabling nodes throughout the world to collaborate on a single training run, Psyche can dramatically reduce the cost of training frontier level models, leveling the playing field for open source AI model developers.
Although we’ve performed extensive scientific research on DisTrO and its implementation in Psyche, we decided to verify its effectiveness for production workloads by training Hermes 4.3 both on Psyche and via the traditional centralized approach. As outlined in the[Hermes 4 Technical Report](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.18255), we used our[custom version of Torchitan](https://github.com/nousresearch/torchtitan/)to train Hermes 4.3 following the same recipe as Hermes 4 (FSDP+AdamW). We then trained the model a second time on Psyche (TP+DisTrO). Given the extended context length and increased training set size, the Hermes 4.3 training run was twice as large as Hermes 4.
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_12-scaled.png)
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_22-scaled.png)
The training run proved stable throughout, averaging 144k tokens/second spread across 24 Psyche nodes. Using DisTro’s overlapped collective strategy, the entirety of the P2P communications were hidden by the training time, effectively achieving equivalent throughput to traditional, centralized training. Under the hood, Psyche uses a dual L1-P2P networking model where consensus state is managed by a smart contract on the Solana blockchain while training gradients are communicated out-of-band through a custom mesh P2P network.
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_34_2-scaled.png)
**The Psyche trained version of Hermes 4.3 outperformed the traditional centralized version on a suite of downstream tasks**. Although previous runs had produced similar results, it was a confirming signal that Psyche is up to the task of training production models. Hermes 4.3 achieves SOTA on RefusalBench across all popular closed and open models in being helpful and aligned to the user’s values. For transparency we are publishing the full set of[evaluation responses and scorings](https://huggingface.co/datasets/NousResearch/eval-Hermes-4.3-36B). In addition we are releasing the centrally trained version ([🤗 Model](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-4.3-36B-centralized),[Evals](https://huggingface.co/datasets/NousResearch/eval-Hermes-4.3-36B-centralized)) as a research artifact (you’re welcome @xl8harder).
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_52-scaled.png)
To learn more about Psyche, check out the[live dashboard](https://psyche.network/)

tags.impact_reason: Artemis providing verifiable data for payment volume on Solana is a solid update that offers insights into real-world usage, but its impact is somewhat limited until the full dashboard launch.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline prism as the Semantic Anchor because it perfectly visualizes Artemis's core value proposition: filtering a chaotic stream of raw on-chain data ('noise') and refracting it into clean, distinct, and valuable insights ('signal'), like the six parallel beams representing B2B payment types. I included the text '6%' as a diegetic holographic projection onto a facet of the prism, directly representing the key data point revealed in the headline.

From the left, a chaotic torrent of glowing, jumbled digital particles representing raw on-chain 'noise' is drawn into the prism. The prism refracts this chaos, emitting six perfectly parallel, coherent beams of pure colored light on the right, representing filtered B2B payment data. A sharp, white holographic number '6%' is projected diegetically onto a facet of the prism, signifying the key finding. The background is dark and deep, with subtle volumetric light catching the outlines of distant server racks.

editorial.headline: Artemis Reveals Solana Captures 6% of On-Chain B2B Payments
editorial.standfirst: New institutional data methodology filters out DeFi noise to reveal the network's actual commercial utility. The findings position Solana as a rising competitor in the $120 billion on-chain payments market.
key_takeaways: Solana now accounts for 5-6% of all on-chain B2B payment volume.
Global on-chain B2B activity is expanding at a 4x year-over-year rate.
Artemis will launch a dedicated TPV Dashboard for institutional tracking in Q1 2026.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For years, institutional valuation of Layer 1 networks has been obfuscated by "fake" volume—arbitrage, MEV, and wash trading. **Artemis**, a data provider serving giants like **Visa** and **Circle**, has introduced a new metric: Total Payment Volume (TPV). By stripping away speculative DeFi flows, Artemis aims to provide the crypto equivalent of **Stripe**'s volume reports, offering the first clear view of blockchains as genuine settlement rails rather than just casinos.

The headline "alpha" delivered at Breakpoint is specific and significant: **Solana** currently processes between 5% and 6% of all on-chain B2B payments. While this figure might seem modest in isolation, it represents high-quality, sticky capital flow in a sector growing 4x year-over-year. This distinguishes Solana from being merely a retail memecoin hub; it is gaining traction in commercial settlements, a vertical previously dominated by legacy fintechs like **Western Union**.

This methodology serves as a critical maturity signal for the asset class. Artemis calculates this data by aggregating flows from payment service providers, payroll processors, and card networks, explicitly excluding trading and airdrops. With the broader on-chain payments market estimated at $120 billion annually, the ability to isolate "real" economic activity is a prerequisite for the next wave of institutional capital allocation.

Looking ahead, Artemis will formalize this tracking with a public TPV dashboard launching in Q1. For investors, this creates a new fundamental KPI: the ability to value Solana not just on gas fees or theoretical throughput, but on its capture of the global remittance and B2B settlement market—a market where it is beginning to challenge incumbents like **Cash App**.
